description_long: This is a rustic log cabin constructed primarily from interlocking wooden logs, featuring a prominent timber frame and exposed truss system. The roof is steeply pitched and supported by visible rafters, with a central gable and additional smaller gable elements extending from the main structure. The cabin includes multiple rectangular window openings distributed along the walls, and at least one main doorway is visible, framed by thick log posts. A covered porch area with a timber post-and-beam structure extends from one side, providing a sheltered entry or outdoor space. The overall design is robust and traditional, evoking classic log cabin architecture with a focus on natural materials and structural simplicity. The wood appears unfinished or lightly treated, giving the cabin a natural, earthy appearance suitable for rural or park-like urban settings.
description: A traditional log cabin with a steep gabled roof, exposed timber framing, multiple window openings, and a covered porch area. The structure is made of interlocking wooden logs and features a rustic, natural finish.
description_view_0: The front gable and main roof structure are prominent, with visible trusses and a central window opening; the porch is partially visible on the right.
description_view_1: This view highlights the main entrance, centered beneath the gable, and shows the arrangement of logs and window openings on the front facade.
description_view_2: The rear gable and roof structure are visible, with the porch area and its supporting posts clearly shown on the left side.
description_view_3: The back of the cabin is shown, with a secondary entrance or window and the porch structure extending from the left; the log construction is clearly visible.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with central and secondary gables, exposed timber trusses, and moderate overhangs
attribute_window_arrangement: multiple rectangular windows of medium size, asymmetrically distributed along the walls, with some grouped and others isolated
attribute_door_placement: one main door centered on the front facade beneath the gable, with a possible secondary entrance at the rear
